metadata.author: Paul
metadata.subject: Christ — The All-inclusive One, Having the Firstplace in All Things as the Mystery and Embodiment of God.

1: Who is the image of the invisible God, the Firstborn of all creation.
To whom God willed to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
2: For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Deity bodily.
And you are made full in Him, who is the Head of all rule and authority.
3: Where there cannot be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, slave, free man, but Christ is all and in all.

27.text: The mystery hidden from the ages is that the all-inclusive Christ now dwells in the believers to be their life today and their glory in the future.

11.text: In the New Man (the church), natural distinctions disappear because Christ is the constituent of every member and the person of the whole Body.
